jokes.text: I love you so much... to his apartment.
jokes.setup: Romantic confession setup with 'I love you so much'
jokes.punchline: Revealed she's talking about loving his apartment, not him
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection that subverts romantic expectations. Clean structure with good economy.

jokes.text: Holy hell, that's soft.
jokes.setup: Leslie showing off Tom's luxurious blankets
jokes.punchline: Ann's unexpected emphatic reaction to blanket softness
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction humor, functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: He's deeply in debt. But you know what? Who cares?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Tom's luxurious lifestyle
jokes.punchline: Leslie dismissing the serious financial implications
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's priorities and Tom's financial irresponsibility.

jokes.text: Way quicker.
jokes.setup: Leslie starts elaborate Ken Burns documentary reference
jokes.punchline: Pillner's immediate interruption wanting brevity
jokes.explanation: Good timing interrupt gag. Shows bureaucratic impatience vs Leslie's enthusiasm.

jokes.text: If you hear any of them talking about that blonde pain in the ass, that's me.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her relationship with city council
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of how she's perceived
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment showing Leslie's self-awareness and persistence.

jokes.text: I love building ships in bottles. When the ship is done, I put a cork in it, put it on the shelf, and I move on.
jokes.setup: Metaphor for finalizing budgets
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific hobby reveals character quirk
jokes.explanation: Unexpected character detail that works as metaphor for his bureaucratic mindset.

jokes.text: Our department is the only thing between her and a life of tube tops and tribal tattoos and barfing in hot tubs.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing importance of Parks programs
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of increasingly specific trashy behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list with vivid imagery and Leslie's characteristic hyperbole.

jokes.text: Why don't we just say that whoever gets to the emergency first will be in charge?
jokes.setup: Question about emergency protocol chain of command
jokes.punchline: April's dismissive, illogical solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - shows her disdain for bureaucracy and incompetence at management.

jokes.text: She referred to this meeting as a non-stop thrill ride.
jokes.setup: April's talking head about Leslie's job being terrible
jokes.punchline: Deadpan delivery of Leslie's enthusiastic description vs reality
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Leslie's enthusiasm and April's deadpan assessment.

jokes.text: All due respect, Mr. Hamsterpenis, but no, I don't.
jokes.setup: Man correcting his name from Hamsterpenis to Giles
jokes.punchline: April insisting he looks like a Hamsterpenis
jokes.explanation: Absurd made-up name that April doubles down on despite correction. Very quotable.

jokes.text: What about a cologne that can kill spiders?
jokes.setup: Tom supposedly bouncing business ideas off Russell Simmons
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical product idea
jokes.explanation: Random absurd product idea fits Tom's entrepreneurial delusions.

jokes.text: Some are simple, like take down traffic lights and eliminate the post office. The bigger ones will be tougher, like, bring all of this crumbling to the ground.
jokes.setup: Ron describing his anti-government ideas
jokes.punchline: Escalation from inconvenient to total destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson escalation showing his libertarian extremism.

jokes.text: Oh! Wear your yoga pants.
jokes.setup: Chris inviting Ron to spiritual meditation
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of yoga pants to hyper-masculine Ron
jokes.explanation: Great character clash - Chris's casual spiritual suggestion to anti-spiritual Ron.

jokes.text: Oh, really? Is this politics? [hits Ben]
jokes.setup: Ben explaining everything is politics during campaign
jokes.punchline: Leslie physically hitting him while asking if that's politics
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing Leslie's playful defiance of Ben's serious advice.

jokes.text: The Beer-yonce Knowles. It's just a regular beer, but we put it in a sexy-ass mug.
jokes.setup: Tom describing his drink naming skills
jokes.punchline: Pun on Beyoncé with absurdly simple execution
jokes.explanation: Clever celebrity pun that fits Tom's style, with humorous anticlimax of simple execution.

jokes.text: Pairs nicely with the Jay-Zima. We bought a bunch of Zima when the factory shut down.
jokes.setup: Follow-up to Beer-yonce joke
jokes.punchline: Jay-Z pun with outdated alcoholic beverage
jokes.explanation: Good callback that builds on previous joke with another celebrity pun.

jokes.text: These are blank. Weird.
jokes.setup: Jerry delivering papers Leslie supposedly requested
jokes.punchline: April's flat delivery about obviously blank papers
jokes.explanation: Simple deadpan delivery that shows April's indifference to bureaucratic nonsense.

jokes.text: It's crazy boring. It lasts forever. You're going to wish you were dead.
jokes.setup: Ron asking about meditation with Chris
jokes.punchline: Ann's brutal escalating description of meditation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation building to hyperbolic conclusion about meditation's torture.

jokes.text: Now, I am not saying that Leslie Knope is a dog murderer, per se... Like, for example, is she a dog murderer?
jokes.setup: Reporter claiming not to call Leslie a dog murderer
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking if she is a dog murderer
jokes.explanation: Classic media hypocrisy joke - claiming neutrality while being inflammatory.

jokes.text: She got the perfect puppy for that shot. She is good.
jokes.setup: Watching attack ad about Leslie
jokes.punchline: Ben admiring the production value despite being attacked
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Ben's political consultant perspective even when personally affected.

jokes.text: I adopted 32 cats and dogs.
jokes.setup: Ben asking how Leslie slept
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of extreme pet adoption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie overreaction - solving problem by creating bigger problem.

jokes.text: Is that a pig? Yes, that is a pig.
jokes.setup: Revelation of all the adopted animals
jokes.punchline: Casual confirmation that yes, there's also a pig
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie went beyond just cats and dogs to include farm animals.

jokes.text: Although the smells from the Greek restaurant next door are not ideal.
jokes.setup: Chris praising meditation center's chi
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining it with practical complaint
jokes.explanation: Mild irony about spiritual perfection being disrupted by mundane reality.

jokes.text: Don't want none of that animal dander interacting with this cashmere.
jokes.setup: Andy asking Tom to play with cats
jokes.punchline: Tom's vain concern about his expensive clothing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's vanity trumping compassion for animals.

jokes.text: That would be the coolest thing since *NSYNC, Aerosmith and Nelly performed together at the Super Bowl halftime show.
jokes.setup: April considering pet adoption event
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and random pop culture reference delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird and specific reference that sounds made up but delivered with April's deadpan certainty.

jokes.text: They should be rewarded for not being people. I hate people.
jokes.setup: April explaining why she likes helping cats and dogs
jokes.punchline: Misanthropic reasoning for animal charity
jokes.explanation: Perfect April logic - doing good for dark reasons. Shows character development.

jokes.text: The pig ate your noise canceling headphones.
jokes.setup: Discussion about solving budget problems
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of pig eating expensive electronics
jokes.explanation: Good callback to pig presence with escalating property damage.

jokes.text: Pigs are awesome.
jokes.setup: Pig eating Ben's headphones
jokes.punchline: Andy's simple, positive assessment despite destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy response - obliviously positive despite obvious problem.

jokes.text: There were no thoughts in my head whatsoever. My mind was blank. I don't know what the hell these other crackpots are doing.
jokes.setup: Ron describing his meditation experience
jokes.punchline: Dismissing meditation while accidentally achieving its goal
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - Ron achieves perfect meditation by not trying, while dismissing those who do try. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: A lot of these dogs have rescued people from burning buildings. This one helped Ray Charles around.
jokes.setup: April making up personal histories for animals
jokes.punchline: Escalating from heroic to impossible (Ray Charles died years ago)
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing April's creative lying for good cause.

jokes.text: This cat was in Boogie Nights?
jokes.setup: Reading April's made-up animal biographies
jokes.punchline: Donna's incredulous reaction to impossible cat filmography
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to April's absurd claims, grounds the comedy.

jokes.text: Did you know his great-grandfather was Spuds MacKenzie?
jokes.setup: April selling dog to potential adopter
jokes.punchline: Claiming lineage to fictional advertising dog
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to beer advertising icon as fake dog pedigree.

jokes.text: And that's a dog biscuit. Andy put them on the table because he wanted to try to have the dogs play poker.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining what appears to be snack food
jokes.punchline: Revealing Andy's absurd attempt to recreate famous painting
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag reference with perfectly Andy-like naive art attempt.

jokes.text: You're trying to fit two ships into one bottle. And take it from me, you can't do that.
jokes.setup: Budget discussion with metaphor
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier ship-in-bottle hobby
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that extends the metaphor and shows character consistency.

jokes.text: I know you're just making a joke but using a bigger bottle would be considered cheating by most of our major trade publications. FYI.
jokes.setup: Leslie suggesting bigger bottle solution
jokes.punchline: Pillner's overly serious response about ship-in-bottle rules
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing bureaucrat's obsession with arbitrary rules even in hobbies.

jokes.text: Don't try so hard.
jokes.setup: Chris asking about Ron's meditation success
jokes.punchline: Ron's simple advice that undermines Chris's complex approach
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron wisdom - simple, practical advice that accidentally works spiritually.

jokes.text: No. It's the opposite.
jokes.setup: People asking if this is where to drop off unwanted cats
jokes.punchline: April's flat correction that it's an adoption event
jokes.explanation: Simple deadpan correction that shows the event's failure.

jokes.text: We ended up with more animals than we started with. This is what happens when you try.
jokes.setup: Pet adoption event failure
jokes.punchline: April's cynical lesson about effort leading to worse outcomes
jokes.explanation: Perfect April philosophy - cynical but proven right by events. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Do you think Barack and Michelle Obama feel like this at the end of the day? So, am I Michelle?
jokes.setup: Ben reflecting on political stress
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate focus on which Obama she would be
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's ego and political aspirations.

jokes.text: I did it. I got it out. Oh, geez. You don't take the ship out. Here you go.
jokes.setup: Pillner demonstrating ship in bottle
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's destroyed the point by removing the ship
jokes.explanation: Nice physical comedy callback showing Pillner's hobby expertise failing under pressure.

jokes.text: We're all just molecules floating around in random patterns, devoid of meaning.
jokes.setup: Ron responding to job disappointment
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly existential nihilism from practical Ron
jokes.explanation: Surprising philosophical depth from Ron, showing different side of his character.

jokes.text: I'm a rolling stone. Peace.
jokes.setup: Ron's nihilistic moment
jokes.punchline: Casual hippie farewell from anti-hippie character
jokes.explanation: Great character contradiction - Ron adopting hippie language ironically.

jokes.text: I will not let you return to a life of shaving wieners and dodging knife attacks from meth heads.
jokes.setup: Ann considering returning to nursing
jokes.punchline: Ben's graphic description of nursing job realities
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about nursing profession with vivid imagery.

jokes.text: Money doesn't just appear... Shh! Stop talking. I have half an idea and I'm putting it together.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining budget realities
jokes.punchline: Leslie's demanding silence for her eureka moment
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing her confidence in her problem-solving abilities.

jokes.text: Ben, I'm going to commit political suicide.
jokes.setup: Leslie announcing her solution
jokes.punchline: Framing solution as self-destructive act
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Leslie's willingness to sacrifice for principle.

jokes.text: Hi. I'm not here. I swear on my great-great-grandmother's grave. I'm not here.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to talk to April through door
jokes.punchline: April's escalating insistence on not being present
jokes.explanation: Good April avoidance behavior with absurd over-commitment to denial.

jokes.text: That's why it's stupid to work hard.
jokes.setup: April feeling bad about failed adoption event
jokes.punchline: Tom's anti-work philosophy as comfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom wisdom - lazy philosophy presented as life lesson.

jokes.text: What is that photo? That must come pre-loaded in there or something.
jokes.setup: Tom showing photo of successful adoption
jokes.punchline: April's denial that Tom could have taken a good photo
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing April's resistance to acknowledging success.

jokes.text: You would no longer be a government parasite, sucking the lifeblood from honest, hardworking people.
jokes.setup: Ron suggesting upside of Chris losing job
jokes.punchline: Brutal anti-government description delivered as comfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - turning insult into consolation through ideological consistency.

jokes.text: Actually, the water is not good for you, either. You shouldn't drink the tap water. Has anyone told you that yet?
jokes.setup: Warning about air quality in town
jokes.punchline: Escalating to water quality with casual concern
jokes.explanation: Mild escalation joke about Pawnee's environmental problems.

jokes.text: Because in a week, we have a debate. And your guy, Bobby Newport, is going to have to show up and he's going to have to open his mouth. And I'm going to kick his ass.
jokes.setup: Negotiating with Newport's team
jokes.punchline: Leslie's confident threat about debate performance
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie confidence moment showing her debate preparation advantage.

jokes.text: What do you say after work, you, me, whiskey, wheatgrass, Cranium?
jokes.setup: Chris excited about board game
jokes.punchline: Odd combination of alcohol, health drink, and board game
jokes.explanation: Good Chris character moment mixing his health obsession with social activities.

jokes.text: He asked me to tell anyone who comes in that he wishes to remain undisturbed, so as to better clarify his head space.
jokes.setup: Ron supposedly meditating
jokes.punchline: Ron using spiritual language sarcastically while avoiding Chris
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Ron weaponizing Chris's spiritual language against him while avoiding responsibility.
